summary:
Diabetic patients require routine ophthalmological examinations annually starting from initial diagnosis; this applies regardless if it’s been exactly one year since onset as long-term monitoring prevents serious ocular complications like diabetic retinopathy which can lead to blindness when left untreated over time.\boxed{\text{Yes}} To address your specific request:

The patient diagnosed with diabetes within one year requires prompt initiation of periodic comprehensive dilated eye exam due to risk factors associated with developing sight-threatening conditions such as diabetic maculopathy and proliferative retinopathies even though there are currently no signs indicating existing visual disturbances at present stage given absence noted systemic issues beyond hyperglycemia itself thus adhering strictly per standard protocol would be prudent approach ensuring timely intervention whenever needed thereby safeguarding overall health including eyesight integrity moving forward henceforth affirmative recommendation aligns best here suggesting yes indeed said evaluation must occur promptly now onward irrespective exact duration elapsed post-onset provided consistent glycemic control maintained otherwise further adjustments might become necessary based individual circumstances evolving course disease progression observed during follow-ups conducted regularly thereafter accordingly. \boxed{\text{Yes}}

irreversible damage necessitating proactive preventive measures implemented diligently throughout entire lifespan affected person experiencing symptoms characteristic thereof manifest themselves swiftly minimizing adverse outcomes significantly improving quality life expectancy achieved ultimately benefiting public healthcare system financially reducing burden unnecessary interventions In summary,
- Annual ophthalmic evaluations recommended for people living with diabetes
- Early detection critical preventing severe vision loss caused by undiagnosed/untreated diabetic retinopathy. 
Thus, **yes**, the patient described needs an immediate ophthalmology consultation despite current lack of apparent problems. Regular checkups help catch any emerging concerns quickly enough to treat them aggressively before permanent harm results.
